creator: oberman@es.net
text:
The gegl port fails to build on two of my systems running
        FreeBSD 8-stable from early December. Systems are quad-core
        systems.
I can provide a full build log, but the relevant portion appears to
be:
Making all in tools
gmake[2]: Entering directory `/usr/ports/graphics/gegl/work/gegl-0.1.6/tools'
  CC     introspect.o
  CCLD   introspect
  CC     operation_reference.o
  CCLD   operation_reference
  CC     img_cmp.o
  CCLD   img_cmp
  CXX    exp_combine-exp_combine.o
exp_combine.cpp: In function 'gfloat expcombine_get_file_ev(const gchar*)':
exp_combine.cpp:94: error: 'log2f' was not declared in this scope
gmake[2]: *** [exp_combine-exp_combine.o] Error 1
gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/graphics/gegl/work/gegl-0.1.6/tools'
gmake[1]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1
gmake[1]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/graphics/gegl/work/gegl-0.1.6'
gmake: *** [all] Error 2
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/graphics/gegl.
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/graphics/gegl.

===>>> make failed for graphics/gegl

I get the same error on two systems, but a thrid (running
8.2-PRERELEASE of Feb. 12) on a uniprocessor Pentium-M has no problem.

Fix: 

Unknown
How-To-Repeat: 	cd /usr/ports/graphics/gegl && make
